content.text:
Here's a daily to-do list for pharmacy operations:

# Daily Pharmacy To-Do List

## Opening Tasks
- [ ] Unlock and disarm security system
- [ ] Turn on computers, registers, and equipment
- [ ] Check refrigerator/freezer temperatures and log readings
- [ ] Review overnight messages and faxes
- [ ] Count cash drawer and verify starting balance

## Prescription Management
- [ ] Process new prescription orders
- [ ] Fill and verify pending prescriptions
- [ ] Check for prescription refill requests
- [ ] Contact prescribers for clarifications or authorizations
- [ ] Address any insurance rejections or prior authorizations

## Customer Service
- [ ] Assist walk-in patients
- [ ] Answer phone inquiries
- [ ] Provide medication counseling
- [ ] Process prescription pick-ups
- [ ] Handle over-the-counter product questions

## Inventory & Stock
- [ ] Check for medication shortages
- [ ] Receive and shelve deliveries
- [ ] Update inventory in system
- [ ] Identify and remove expired medications
- [ ] Place orders for low-stock items

## Administrative
- [ ] Verify insurance claims
- [ ] File completed paperwork
- [ ] Update patient records
- [ ] Review and resolve pending issues

## Closing Tasks
- [ ] Reconcile cash drawer
- [ ] Complete daily sales report
- [ ] Secure controlled substances
- [ ] Clean and organize work areas
- [ ] Arm security system and lock up

---

Feel free to let me know if you'd like me to customize this list for a specific type of pharmacy (retail, hospital, compounding, etc.) or adjust it based on particular responsibilities!
